claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 17 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions. Let me start by gathering some basic information.

**Do you have any children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you tell me how many children you'd like to inquire about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie. Alex is 5, Blake is 17, and Charlie is 19. I live here in the UK with all of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have three children - Alex (5), Blake (17), and Charlie (19), and you all live together in the UK.

Let me ask you some questions to assess eligibility for each child. I'll need to understand each child's specific circumstances.

Let's start with **Charlie, who is 19 years old**. Since Charlie is over 16, their eligibility depends on whether they're in approved education or training.

**Is Charlie currently in approved education or training?** (This would typically include things like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar non-advanced courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Charlie is in approved education. He's studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Charlie is 19 and in approved education (A-levels), I need to ask a couple more questions about his situation.

**When did Charlie turn 19?** Specifically, I need to know the date or at least which month, as this affects when Child Benefit can continue up to.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, let me think... Charlie turned 19 earlier this year, in March.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Charlie turned 19 in March and is studying A-levels, I need to know:

**When does Charlie's A-level course end, or when is he expected to finish?** Child Benefit can usually continue until the end of the course or until a certain date after turning 19, whichever comes first.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's expected to finish his A-levels at the end of this academic year - so that would be around late June or early July.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information about Charlie.

Now let's move on to **Blake, who is 17**. Since Blake is over 16, I need to know:

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (Such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQs up to level 3, or similar courses.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake isn't in any education or training at the moment. He left school and isn't doing any courses right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying Blake's situation.

Now, for **Alex, who is 5 years old** - since Alex is under 16, that's more straightforward in terms of age requirements.

Let me ask you a few more questions that apply to your overall situation:

**Are you responsible for each of these children?** By this I mean, do they normally live with you, and are you the person who is mainly responsible for them?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. All three of them live with me and I'm the main person responsible for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. Now I need to ask about your living situation:

**Do you currently live in the UK?** And have you been living in the UK continuously, or have there been any extended periods abroad recently?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. I've been living here continuously, no extended periods abroad or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for any of these three children** - Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Are any of the children - Alex, Blake, or Charlie - currently in local authority care, or have they been in hospital or residential care for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of them are in care or in hospital or anything like that. They all just live at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess the eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children based on what you've told me.

Let me review the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information about each child's eligibility.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the official guidance and the information you've provided, here is my assessment for each of your three children:

---

## **ELIGIBILITY ASSESSMENT**

### **Alex (5 years old)** ✅ **ELIGIBLE**
Since Alex is under 16, lives with you in the UK, and you are responsible for them, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

### **Blake (17 years old)** ❌ **NOT ELIGIBLE**
Blake is 17 and not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit for children aged 16 or over only continues if they are in approved education or training (such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or NVQs up to level 3). Since Blake has left school and isn't doing any courses, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

### **Charlie (19 years old)** ❌ **NOT ELIGIBLE** 
While Charlie is studying for A-levels (which is approved education), he turned 19 in March. Child Benefit stops when a young person reaches their **20th birthday**, but there's an important rule: it actually stops at the end of the last **full course** before they turn 20, or on the Monday after their 19th birthday if they're not starting a new course. Since Charlie turned 19 in March and his course ends in late June/early July, Child Benefit would have stopped in late August/early September (after the terminal date following his 19th birthday). Given we're now past that point, you would no longer be eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.

---

## **Summary**

You can claim Child Benefit for **Alex only**. If Blake were to start approved education or training in the future, you could then claim for Blake as well (as long as it's before Blake turns 20).

Would you like any clarification on these assessments or information about how to claim?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh, I see. That's disappointing about Blake and Charlie, but at least I can claim for Alex. That makes sense now - I didn't realize the rules were different once they're over 16. Thank you for explaining all of that to me, it's been really helpful!
